scenario_title: Duplicate add_rr of the same RR in one UPDATE
test_rationale: This test targets the underspecified case where a single DNS UPDATE contains two identical add_rr operations for the same owner, type, class, TTL, and RDATA. The zone is pre-seeded with the target RR so the server must decide whether the duplicated additions are silently coalesced, treated as an idempotent no-op, or rejected as an error. That decision is externally visible both in the UPDATE reply code and in the post-update RRset observed by QUERY, making it suitable for a binary oracle between BIND and Knot.

group_1.behavior: Accepts the UPDATE by suppressing the duplicate add or treating it as idempotent; the queried RRset remains present as a single A RR.
group_1.post_state: dup.campus.edu. has exactly one A RR with 198.51.100.7

group_2.behavior: Rejects the UPDATE when it detects the duplicate identical add_rr in the same message, surfacing an error such as YXRRSET-like failure semantics or equivalent update rejection.
group_2.post_state: dup.campus.edu. remains unchanged with one A RR with 198.51.100.7

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.3 defines add semantics but does not specify whether duplicate identical add_rr operations within a single UPDATE must be deduplicated, treated as a no-op, or rejected. RFC 2181 §5.2 says identical RRs are meaningless and should be suppressed, but it does not define UPDATE error handling for intra-message duplicates, leaving servers free to choose different reply codes and/or acceptance behavior.
